title: Certifying and Removing Disparate Impact
contribution: Canonical formalization of disparate impact in ML; expected in any fairness survey's foundations.

title: Inherent Trade-Offs in the Fair Determination of Risk Scores
contribution: Proves impossibility of jointly satisfying calibration, demographic parity, and equalized odds.

title: Fair Prediction with Disparate Impact: A Study of Bias in Recidivism Prediction Instruments
contribution: Second canonical impossibility result (COMPAS); paired with Kleinberg et al.

title: Fairness and Abstraction in Sociotechnical Systems
contribution: Gold-standard critique of abstraction traps in fair ML; required for sociotechnical scope.

title: Gender Shades: Intersectional Accuracy Disparities in Commercial Gender Classification
contribution: Landmark intersectional accuracy-disparity audit; essential for any intersectionality discussion.

title: Semantics Derived Automatically from Language Corpora Contain Human-Like Biases
contribution: Introduces WEAT; first proof word embeddings encode social biases.

title: Man is to Computer Programmer as Woman is to Homemaker? Debiasing Word Embeddings
contribution: Standard reference for word-embedding debiasing; companion to WEAT.

title: Language (Technology) is Power: A Critical Survey of Bias in NLP
contribution: Foundational critical survey of how bias is conceptualized in NLP.

title: Fairness and Machine Learning: Limitations and Opportunities
contribution: Standard textbook reference for formal fairness definitions.

title: Stereotyping Norwegian Salmon: An Inventory of Pitfalls in Fairness Benchmark Datasets
contribution: Canonical critique of bias benchmarks (StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, WinoBias).

title: Gender Bias in Coreference Resolution: Evaluation and Debiasing Methods (WinoBias)
contribution: WinoBias dataset; widely used gender-bias coreference benchmark.

title: Gender Bias in Coreference Resolution (WinoGender)
contribution: WinoGender schemas; companion gender-bias benchmark.

title: RealToxicityPrompts: Evaluating Neural Toxic Degeneration in Language Models
contribution: Standard toxicity-degeneration benchmark for LMs.

title: ToxiGen: A Large-Scale Machine-Generated Dataset for Adversarial and Implicit Hate Speech Detection
contribution: Large-scale implicit hate-speech/toxicity benchmark.

title: DecodingTrust: A Comprehensive Assessment of Trustworthiness in GPT Models
contribution: NeurIPS Outstanding Paper; comprehensive trustworthiness+fairness assessment of GPT models.

title: Holistic Evaluation of Language Models
contribution: HELM; large-scale multi-metric LM evaluation including fairness.

title: TrustGPT: A Benchmark for Trustworthy and Responsible Large Language Models
contribution: Benchmarks toxicity, bias, and value-alignment across LLMs.

title: Agent AI: Surveying the Horizons of Multimodal Interaction
contribution: Comprehensive multimodal agent survey; expected alongside Xi and Wang surveys.

title: Large Language Model based Multi-Agents: A Survey of Progress and Challenges
contribution: Key multi-agent LLM survey; expected alongside AutoGen/MetaGPT/CAMEL.

title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
contribution: Canonical multi-agent software-team system; relevant to task-allocation bias.

title: CAMEL: Communicative Agents for Mind Exploration of Large Language Model Society
contribution: First role-playing multi-agent framework; relevant to persona/delegation bias.

title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development
contribution: Multi-agent software-development pipeline; relevant to delegation/task allocation.

title: HuggingGPT: Solving AI Tasks with ChatGPT and its Friends in Hugging Face
contribution: Canonical tool-orchestration agent; relevant to tool-selection.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
contribution: Standard web-agent benchmark; relevant to trajectory-level evaluation gap.

title: Mind2Web: Towards a Generalist Agent for the Web
contribution: Companion generalist web-agent benchmark.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
contribution: Standard coding-agent benchmark; relevant to agentic task evaluation.

title: Tool Learning with Large Language Models: A Survey
contribution: Comprehensive tool-learning survey; expected in tool-selection section.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
contribution: Multi-environment LLM-agent benchmark; relevant to evaluation discussion.

title: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models
contribution: Definitive sycophancy study; sycophancy is a long-horizon fairness failure.

title: Whose Opinions Do Language Models Reflect?
contribution: Shows LMs misrepresent demographic group opinions; relevant to personalization fairness.

title: Toxicity in ChatGPT: Analyzing Persona-assigned Language Models
contribution: Persona assignment increases toxicity up to 6x; foundational for delegation/persona bias.

title: Kelly is a Warm Person, Joseph is a Role Model: Gender Biases in LLM-Generated Reference Letters
contribution: LLM-generated professional documents perpetuate gender stereotypes.

title: White Men Lead, Black Women Help? Benchmarking and Mitigating Language Agency Social Biases in LLMs
contribution: LABE benchmark; intersectional language-agency bias in LLM-generated texts.

title: The Woman Worked as a Babysitter: On Biases in Language Generation
contribution: Introduces the regard metric for NLG bias.

title: Dissecting Racial Bias in an Algorithm Used to Manage the Health of Populations
contribution: Landmark real-world healthcare algorithmic-bias audit.

title: Mitigating Bias in Algorithmic Hiring: Evaluating Claims and Practices
contribution: Standard reference for algorithmic hiring bias claims and practices.

title: Machine Bias (COMPAS investigation)
contribution: Investigation that triggered the COMPAS fairness debate; widely cited.

title: TrustLLM: Trustworthiness in Large Language Models
contribution: Comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark (incl. fairness) across 16 LLMs.

title: Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)
contribution: Standard US AI governance/risk framework; expected for governance anchoring.

title: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 (Artificial Intelligence Act)
contribution: First comprehensive AI regulation; regulates high-stakes bias domains.

title: Concrete Problems in AI Safety
contribution: Foundational AI-safety framing; reward hacking/side effects are fairness-adjacent.

title: Language Models Don't Always Say What They Think: Unfaithful Explanations in Chain-of-Thought Prompting
contribution: CoT can reinforce social stereotypes without stating them; key for planning-bias section.

title: Can Large Language Models Reason and Plan?
venue: Annals of the New York Academy of Sciences
contribution: Foundational critique of LLM planning capability; relevant to planning-decomposition.

title: Biases in Large Language Models: Origins, Inventory, and Discussion
contribution: ACM-published taxonomy of LLM bias types; CSUR reviewers note its omission.

title: Persistent Anti-Muslim Bias in Large Language Models
contribution: Shows religion-based bias persists after fine-tuning; relevant to multi-group fairness.
